opp.name: CCM_RFP P2P + Sourcing _Opp_May'26
opp.next_step: Maintain engagement with Ryan, gather more details around the RFP scope and timeline, and position Zycus ahead of formal evaluation.
opp.ais_why:
* The engagement is pre-RFP: Chase Cost Management is "preparing to release an RFP for a Source to Order initiative" after meeting Zycus at a procurement event, with head of procurement Ryan confirming interest.
* Nothing in the record points to AI interest: this is a pre-RFP Source-to-Order modernization ("preparing to release an RFP for a Source to Order initiative"), and product scope is iSource and eProcurement only - no Merlin/AI modules attached.
* Drivers are efficiency, control and structured procurement automation via a formal RFP (competitors Coupa/GEP/JAGGAER/SAP Ariba) - a conventional sourcing evaluation, not an AI-led one. Early stage (Qualified, 10%).

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or pain metrics identified across any source. The opportunity name references 'RFP P2P + Sourcing' which implies a procurement transformation scope, but no calls, tasks, or Salesforce fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c) carry buyer-stated cost savings, headcount reduction, cycle-time improvement, or ROI target. Without buyer engagement we have no line of sight to what problem they are solving, the scale of inefficiency, or the financial case that would drive a six-figure P2P + Sourcing purchase. The immediate move is to re-engage Ryan Bradford and surface the quantified pain in the first conversation (current spend under management, manual process cost, sourcing cycle time, compliance risk).

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Ryan Bradford (Decision Maker), no Champion role
SF X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Champion_Identified__c: null or false
Zero Avoma calls or Tasks showing advocacy behavior
champion.narrative: No champion identified. Ryan Bradford is the sole contact role (Decision Maker) but there is zero evidence of advocacy, internal coaching, or active engagement. No calls exist to show him driving the agenda, sharing internal docs, pushing back on competitors for Zycus, or introducing us to the Economic Buyer or broader committee. Salesforce X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c and Champion_Identified__c are null or false. With no buyer engagement on record, we cannot assess his strength, access to power, or willingness to champion. The synthesis read is that we have a single point of contact in a Decision Maker role, but no champion behavior and no multi-threading to derisk single-thread dependency. The immediate move is to re-engage Ryan, assess his advocacy and access, and secure introductions to the EB and at least two other evaluation stakeholders within 14 days. If Ryan cannot or will not multi-thread, escalate to find another internal advocate.

sources: Zero Avoma calls to confirm buyer shortlist or competitive positioning
competition.narrative: Four competitors named in Salesforce Competitors__c field (Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba), but zero call evidence of active competitive dynamics, buyer shortlist, or bake-off structure. No Avoma calls exist to confirm which rivals are live, what the buyer's evaluation criteria favor, or how Zycus is positioned. Salesforce Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c are null. The competitor field appears to be a rep's initial assessment (possibly from the RFP mention or market knowledge) rather than buyer-confirmed intelligence. Without buyer engagement we do not know if this is a true four-way bake-off, a sole-source with Zycus, or if one competitor (GEP as a size/fit peer, or Coupa/Ariba as enterprise incumbents) is already leading. The wedge and differentiation strategy cannot be built without knowing what the buyer values and which rival is closest to their fit. Re-engage to discover the true competitive shortlist, their evaluation status, and what Zycus must prove to win.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is unconfirmed. The opportunity name ('P2P + Sourcing') implies procurement inefficiency (manual requisitioning, fragmented supplier base, lack of spend visibility, slow sourcing cycles), but no buyer-stated pain exists in any call, task, or Salesforce field. Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Compelling_Event__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c are null. We do not know if they are replacing an incumbent system (Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c null), addressing a compliance gap, or solving a cost-control mandate from partners. No Avoma calls captured the operational or financial pain owner. Without confirmed pain and a compelling event, the urgency to close by 16 Sep is speculative. The re-engagement call must extract the pain (what is broken today, the cost of inaction, the trigger for this RFP) and name the pain owner.

paper_process.narrative: Paper process and contracting mechanics are unknown. No MSA, SOW, legal-review timeline, InfoSec questionnaire, or procurement-channel visibility. Salesforce Current_Contract_Expiration__c is null (no incumbent forcing function), and no fields or calls describe whether Chase Cost Management runs vendor onboarding through a procurement team, requires InfoSec certification, or has standard contract templates. No SI or implementation partner is identified. For a professional-services consultancy, data security (client matter confidentiality) and ERP integration (likely a legacy or mid-market finance system) are probable gates, but we have no evidence of either being scoped. With 82 days to close and zero paper-process visibility, the timeline is at high risk. Surface contracting path, legal review lead-time, and InfoSec requirements in the re-engagement.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Ryan Bradford (Decision Maker), no Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor role
economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is not identified. Ryan Bradford holds the Decision Maker contact role (Head of Procurement) but no Executive Sponsor, CFO, or budget-authority contact role exists, and no Avoma calls show who controls commercial approval or pricing negotiation. Salesforce fields X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c are all null or unpopulated. For a $100k P2P + Sourcing deal at an AM Law 50 firm consultancy, budget authority likely sits with a CFO, COO, or the President (account contact list shows Natalya Berdzenishvili as President, Daniel Griffiths as VP of Technology). The synthesis read finds no evidence of EB engagement. Map the EB in the re-engagement call and secure a direct conversation within 14 days.

decision_process.narrative: Decision process is unmapped. No call sequence, no stakeholder escalation pattern, and no approval-chain visibility. Salesforce What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c are null. The single contact role (Ryan Bradford, Decision Maker) suggests he owns the evaluation, but we do not know if this is an RFP with a formal committee, a sole-source selection, or a multi-approval chain involving finance, legal, IT, and executive sign-off. For an AM Law 50 consultancy a P2P + Sourcing purchase likely requires CFO and possibly board or partner approval. With zero buyer engagement we have no read on timeline, gate structure, or who joins at each stage. The re-engagement call must surface the formal process, approval steps, and the realistic path to signature by 16 Sep.

sources: Opp name references RFP but no RFP document or criteria on file
decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria unknown. The opportunity name signals an RFP is expected ('CCM_RFP P2P + Sourcing'), and NextStep mentions 'gather more details around the RFP scope', but no RFP document, requirements list, evaluation scorecard, or buyer-stated must-haves have been captured. Salesforce fields Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c, Shoe_Fit_Criteria_Met__c are null. No Avoma calls exist to extract use-case priorities or technical fit requirements. We do not know whether AI/Merlin capability, ERP integration depth, supplier network breadth, user experience, or total cost of ownership will drive the decision. Secure the RFP or a structured requirements brief in the re-engagement, and run a discovery call to map their evaluation framework.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI fit signal is unknown. No AIS score or status on record, and no Avoma calls to assess buyer AI appetite (AI Hungry vs Curious vs Resistant). The opportunity name and scope (RFP P2P + Sourcing for a professional-services consultancy) suggest procurement efficiency and cost control are priorities, which align with Merlin AI use cases (sourcing automation, supplier negotiation, spend analytics), but we have no evidence the buyer has articulated AI appetite or innovation mandate. Tier cannot be determined without discovery.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 22 May 2026 (35 days ago) and has remained in Qualified stage with no progression. NextStep last modified 18 Jun 2026 (8 days ago) describes seller intent to engage Ryan and gather RFP details, but no buyer action or reply is evidenced. No stage movement, no amount change, no close-date slip, and no completed activities in field history or task log. The deal is stationary.

items.detail: Economic Buyer unmapped. No EB contact role, no executive sponsor, no budget-authority visibility. For a $100k deal, CFO or President sign-off is likely required, but we have no relationship or line of sight to that level. Single-threading through Ryan Bradford (Decision Maker, Head of Procurement) without EB access is a close-risk vulnerability.

Single-thread dependency and ghost risk. Only one buyer-side contact role (Ryan Bradford). No multi-threading to EB, Champion, or broader committee. If Ryan goes dark, leaves, or cannot advocate, the deal dies. No engagement evidence since opp creation 22 May 2026 (35 days ago) increases ghost-risk severity.

82 days to close (16 Sep 2026) with zero MEDDPICC foundation, no RFP visibility, no discovery, and no buyer engagement. If the RFP is issued in the next 2-4 weeks without Zycus positioning, we will be responding blind. Even if we re-engage immediately, building MEDDPICC, running demos, navigating an RFP process, pricing, and contracting in 82 days is aggressive for a cold start.

Competitive landscape unconfirmed. Four competitors named (Coupa, GEP, JAGGAER, Ariba) but zero buyer intelligence on shortlist, bake-off structure, or our positioning. We do not know if we are in a true four-way RFP, a sole-source, or already eliminated. Blind competitive positioning is a win-probability vulnerability.

items.expected_effect: Restart buyer relationship, confirm the RFP is live and timeline is realistic, and secure a 60-minute discovery call within 14 days to build MEDDPICC foundation. If no reply within 10 business days, pivot to map alternative stakeholder (President or VP of Technology from account contacts) or disqualify the opportunity.

items.expected_effect: Identify the Economic Buyer (likely CFO or President), understand their priorities and budget-approval authority, and secure a direct conversation to validate the business case and close timeline. Derisk single-thread dependency and build executive alignment required for commercial sign-off.

items.expected_effect: Extract quantified pain and compelling event (current spend under management, manual-process cost, sourcing cycle time, compliance gaps). items.action: Multi-thread to at least two additional stakeholders beyond Ryan Bradford.
items.trigger: Single buyer-side contact role (Ryan Bradford, Decision Maker). No EB, no Champion, no multi-threading to evaluation committee or executive sponsor. For a $100k deal, evaluation likely involves finance, IT, procurement, and executive sign-off. Single-threading is a ghost risk (if Ryan goes dark or cannot advocate, deal dies).
items.expected_effect: Derisk single-point dependency by securing introductions to the Economic Buyer (CFO or President) and at least one evaluation influencer (finance lead, IT/systems owner, or another procurement stakeholder). Add all discovered contacts as OpportunityContactRoles. Build multiple advocacy threads to survive if primary contact goes dark.

items.action: Position Merlin AI narrative and gauge buyer AI appetite in discovery call.
items.trigger: No AIS score or status, no Avoma calls to assess buyer AI appetite. Opp scope (P2P + Sourcing) is natural fit for Merlin AI-driven sourcing automation, supplier intelligence, negotiation agents. Competitors (Coupa, GEP, JAGGAER, Ariba) have static or bolt-on AI modules. Merlin differentiation is unbuilt and likely under-positioned (no pitch = no traction).
items.expected_effect: Lead discovery call with Merlin value proposition (AI negotiation agent on supplier price history, sourcing cycle automation, spend intelligence). Gauge buyer reaction (AI Hungry vs Curious vs Resistant) and tailor demo and RFP response to emphasize AI differentiation vs competitors. Position Merlin as the wedge to win the RFP.

confidence_signals.summary: No confidence signals exist. Zero buyer engagement, no calls, no tasks, no demonstrated champion behavior, no EB mapped, no MEDDPICC coverage, and no forward momentum since opp creation 22 May 2026. The deal is cold and confidence in the 16 Sep close is effectively zero without immediate re-engagement and full discovery.

Zero verified buyer engagement (LastActivityDate null). No field-history stage movement, no submission-date series populated (Qualified_Submission_Date__c, Formal_Eval_Submission_Date__c, Shortlisted_Submission_Date__c all null). NextStep last modified 18 Jun (8 days ago) is seller intent, not buyer action. 35 days in Qualified with no progression and no buyer signal is a stall.

north_star_verdict.headline: 35 days in Qualified with zero buyer engagement—no calls, tasks, or activities recorded. Single-threaded to Ryan Bradford with no EB or Champion. Named RFP shows no submission evidence or procurement activity. Deal is cold and unvalidated.

best_practice_check.summary: Immediate re-engagement required to restart a cold deal. Multi-thread to derisk single-point dependency and map the Economic Buyer. Run full discovery to build MEDDPICC foundation (pain, metrics, criteria, process, champion) before the RFP formalizes.

competitive_position.summary: Four competitors named in Salesforce (Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba) but with zero buyer-engagement evidence, the competitive landscape is unconfirmed speculation. No calls, tasks, or next-step history reference any rival's pricing, feature comparison, or buyer preference. All four competitors are marked 'active' by default from the SF field (logged at opp creation or shortly after), but true threat level and current status are unknown without buyer discovery. GEP Worldwide is named as primary competitor (likely a size/fit peer for mid-market P2P + Sourcing), but no evidence supports this ranking over Coupa (enterprise SaaS leader, high brand recognition) or Ariba (SAP incumbent advantage in ERP-integrated accounts). The single strongest current threat cannot be determined without re-engagement. The competitive wedge (Merlin AI negotiation agent, supplier intelligence, user experience vs legacy Ariba, or total cost vs Coupa) is unbuilt.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.how_we_win: Unknown without buyer discovery. Likely wedge: Merlin AI-driven sourcing + negotiation automation vs GEP's traditional suite, user experience, and faster time-to-value.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.how_we_win: Unknown without buyer discovery. Likely wedge: Total cost of ownership (Coupa high price point), Merlin AI + sourcing depth, and consultative fit for professional-services procurement vs Coupa's enterprise scale focus.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.how_we_win: Unknown without buyer discovery. Likely wedge: User experience and configurability vs Ariba's legacy UI, Merlin AI innovation vs static Ariba modules, and agility vs SAP's slow release cycles.

competitors.name: JAGGAER
competitors.how_we_win: Unknown without buyer discovery. Likely wedge: Merlin AI + modern UX vs JAGGAER's legacy platform, and Zycus sourcing + contract intelligence depth.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is unbuilt. No AIS score, no AIS status, no Avoma calls to assess buyer AI appetite or Merlin narrative traction. The opportunity scope (P2P + Sourcing) is a natural fit for Merlin AI-driven sourcing automation, supplier intelligence, and negotiation agents, but we have not positioned it. With competitors like Coupa, GEP, JAGGAER, and Ariba in the mix, AI differentiation (Merlin vs their static or bolt-on AI modules) could be the wedge, but it has not been introduced. AI positioning strength is unknown and likely under-positioned by default (no pitch = no traction). The re-engagement call must lead with the Merlin value proposition (AI negotiation agent on supplier price history, sourcing cycle automation, spend intelligence) and gauge buyer reaction.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Customer expectations and fit are unconfirmed. The opportunity name signals P2P and Sourcing scope, and products logged (iSource, eProcurement) align with that scope, but no discovery calls, RFP document, or buyer-stated requirements exist to validate fit. We do not know their must-haves (AI/Merlin appetite, ERP integration requirements, supplier network breadth, user adoption priorities, mobile access, analytics depth) or their deal-breakers. No gaps or misalignments have been identified because no expectations have been captured.

* Drivers are efficiency, control and structured procurement automation via a formal RFP (competitors Coupa/GEP/JAGGAER/SAP Ariba) - a conventional sourcing evaluation, not an AI-led one. Early stage (Qualified, 10%).

deltas.subject: RE-ENGAGE IMMEDIATELY: Deal is 35 days old with zero verified buyer engagement (no LastActivityDate, no calls, no tasks). Ryan Bradford (Decision Maker contact role) is the sole thread and has not responded or engaged since opp creation on 22 May 2026. With 82 days to a 16 Sep close and an expected RFP, the window to position Zycus ahead of formal evaluation is closing. The rank-1 move is for Justin Ajmo (deal owner) to re-engage Ryan directly within 7 days via multi-channel outreach (email + LinkedIn + phone) to confirm the RFP timeline, surface the pain and compelling event, and secure a discovery call. If no reply within 10 business days, escalate to map an alternative stakeholder (the President or VP of Technology from account contacts) or disqualify.

deltas.subject: RUN FULL DISCOVERY TO the relevant stakeholder BEFORE RFP FORMALIZES: The opportunity name signals an RFP is coming ('CCM_RFP P2P + Sourcing'), but we have zero MEDDPICC coverage (no Pain, no Metrics, no Economic Buyer, no Decision Criteria, no Decision Process, no Champion). If the RFP is issued before Zycus runs discovery and positions the solution, we will be responding blind to a buyer-controlled evaluation framework likely shaped by a competitor or the incumbent. The re-engagement must secure a 60-minute discovery call within 14 days to: extract the quantified pain and compelling event (current spend under management, manual-process cost, sourcing cycle time, compliance gaps), map the Economic Buyer and approval chain, understand the decision criteria and RFP scope (AI/Merlin appetite, ERP integration, supplier network, analytics), confirm the competitive shortlist and our differentiation wedge (Merlin AI negotiation agent, user experience, total cost vs Coupa/Ariba), and identify or develop a Champion who will coach us through the RFP and advocate for Zycus internally.

value.detail: Economic Buyer unmapped. No EB contact role, no executive sponsor, no budget-authority visibility. For a $100k deal, CFO or President sign-off is likely required, but we have no relationship or line of sight to that level. Single-threading through Ryan Bradford (Decision Maker, Head of Procurement) without EB access is a close-risk vulnerability.

value.detail: 82 days to close (16 Sep 2026) with zero MEDDPICC foundation, no RFP visibility, no discovery, and no buyer engagement. If the RFP is issued in the next 2-4 weeks without Zycus positioning, we will be responding blind. Even if we re-engage immediately, building MEDDPICC, running demos, navigating an RFP process, pricing, and contracting in 82 days is aggressive for a cold start.

value.flag: RE-ENGAGE IMMEDIATELY: Deal is 35 days old with zero verified buyer engagement (no LastActivityDate, no calls, no tasks). Ryan Bradford (Decision Maker contact role) is the sole thread and has not responded or engaged since opp creation on 22 May 2026. With 82 days to a 16 Sep close and an expected RFP, the window to position Zycus ahead of formal evaluation is closing. The rank-1 move is for Justin Ajmo (deal owner) to re-engage Ryan directly within 7 days via multi-channel outreach (email + LinkedIn + phone) to confirm the RFP timeline, surface the pain and compelling event, and secure a discovery call. If no reply within 10 business days, escalate to map an alternative stakeholder (the President or VP of Technology from account contacts) or disqualify.

value.flag: MULTI-THREAD TO the relevant stakeholder: Only one buyer-side contact role exists (Ryan Bradford, Decision Maker). No Economic Buyer, no Champion, no multi-threading to the broader evaluation committee or executive sponsor. For a $100k P2P + Sourcing deal at a professional-services consultancy, budget authority likely sits with the CFO or President, and the evaluation likely involves finance, IT, and procurement stakeholders. Single-threading through Ryan is a ghost risk (if he leaves, goes dark, or cannot advocate, the deal dies). In the re-engagement call, secure introductions to at least two additional stakeholders: the Economic Buyer (CFO or President) and one evaluation influencer (finance lead, IT/systems owner, or another procurement stakeholder). Add all discovered contacts as OpportunityContactRoles within 14 days.

value.flag: RUN FULL DISCOVERY TO the relevant stakeholder BEFORE RFP FORMALIZES: The opportunity name signals an RFP is coming ('CCM_RFP P2P + Sourcing'), but we have zero MEDDPICC coverage (no Pain, no Metrics, no Economic Buyer, no Decision Criteria, no Decision Process, no Champion). If the RFP is issued before Zycus runs discovery and positions the solution, we will be responding blind to a buyer-controlled evaluation framework likely shaped by a competitor or the incumbent. The re-engagement must secure a 60-minute discovery call within 14 days to: extract the quantified pain and compelling event (current spend under management, manual-process cost, sourcing cycle time, compliance gaps), map the Economic Buyer and approval chain, understand the decision criteria and RFP scope (AI/Merlin appetite, ERP integration, supplier network, analytics), confirm the competitive shortlist and our differentiation wedge (Merlin AI negotiation agent, user experience, total cost vs Coupa/Ariba), and identify or develop a Champion who will coach us through the RFP and advocate for Zycus internally.

gaps: Zero Avoma calls discovered via opp + account + attendee-email discovery (22 May - 26 Jun window). No buyer engagement evidence from any source.
Zero completed Tasks or Events in 90-day window (SF Task/Event query returned zero).
No field-history movement on Stage, Amount, or CloseDate since creation (SF OpportunityFieldHistory returned creation event only).

All MEDDPICC synthesis-map fields null or unpopulated (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c, X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, What_is_the_decision_process__c, X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Decision_Maker_Name_Title__c).
No AIS score, status, or why populated (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c null).
No submission-date series populated (Qualified_Submission_Date__c, Formal_Eval_Submission_Date__c, Shortlisted_Submission_Date__c null) so true time-in-stage cannot be computed from dates.
Single buyer-side contact role (Ryan Bradford); no EB, no Champion, no multi-threading contacts.
Competitors named in SF field (Coupa, GEP, JAGGAER, Ariba) but zero call or task evidence of buyer shortlist, bake-off structure, or competitive positioning.
